source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two objects, A and B, are connected by hinges to a rigid rod. The objects slide along perpendicular guide rails as shown in figure. Assume object A slides to the left with a constant speed. Find the velocity \( v_\text{B} \) of object B as a function of the angle \( \theta \).

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
the needed geometry depends on the depicted perpendicular rails, rigid rod, and angle theta placement
multi_step: the setup combines a geometric constraint (rigid rod between sliders) with motion information (object A moves at constant speed)
finding v_B as a function of theta requires relating the two slider coordinates/velocities through the rod-angle geometry
this is more than direct lookup and likely involves at least a geometry step plus a kinematics/rate relation step
verifiability: the target answer is a compact symbolic expression that can be checked against algebraically equivalent forms
multiple-choice options are provided, giving an additional discrete verification route

decision_reasons: core assets are complete: question text, primary image, and answer are all present
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scoring is strong overall, with high image dependency and verifiability
the problem appears recoverable and usable as a geometry/kinematics item requiring the figure
risk flags are manageable verification concerns rather than missing-semantic issues
